provenance: Fetched from official Anthropic / Claude Code docs by the cc-cost-research workflow (10 Sonnet agents). Not from model memory.

models.notes: Anthropic's most capable widely released model as of June 2026. Generally available on Claude API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry.
Limited availability via Project Glasswing (invitation-only). Same pricing as Fable 5.
Current flagship Opus model. 1M token context window. Fast mode available at $10/$50 MTok input/output.
Best combination of speed and intelligence. 1M token context window. Supports extended thinking and adaptive thinking.
Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence. 200k token context window. Supports extended thinking.

pricing.ratios: Input cost ratios (per MTok): Fable 5 / Mythos 5 = $10 (2x Opus 4.8, 3.33x Sonnet 4.6, 10x Haiku 4.5). Opus 4.8 = $5 (1.67x Sonnet 4.6, 5x Haiku 4.5). Sonnet 4.6 = $3 (3x Haiku 4.5). Haiku 4.5 = $1 (baseline). Output cost ratios follow the same pattern: Fable 5/Mythos 5 at $50 is 2x Opus ($25), 3.33x Sonnet ($15), 10x Haiku ($5). Cache read ratios (0.1x base input price for all models): Fable/Mythos $1, Opus 4.8 $0.50, Sonnet 4.6 $0.30, Haiku 4.5 $0.10 — so a cached Haiku call costs 1/100th of a non-cached Fable call. This 10x spread between tiers on input (and matched on output, cache write, cache read) means Haiku with prompt caching ($0.10/MTok reads) is 100x cheaper per cached token than Fable 5 at base input rates ($10/MTok).

summary: The biggest quota-waste levers in Claude Code are: (1) running the wrong model tier — Opus/Fable at 5-10x Haiku input cost on routine work, and subagents inheriting an expensive parent model (agent teams burn ~7x tokens) — fixed by model/effortLevel/CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL pins; (2) cache misses, where extended thinking (billed as output, tens of thousands of tokens/turn), mid-session model/effort switches, and MCP-server churn force full-context reprocessing at 1x instead of 0.1x cache-read rates; (3) per-turn context bloat from oversized CLAUDE.md, undeferred MCP tool schemas (~55K tokens for a 5-server setup), and verbose tool/log output dumped into the main thread instead of filtered by hooks or isolated in subagents; and (4) over-large context windows (1M variants) and 1h-cache writes (2x premium) used when the session never amortizes them. Most savings come from a handful of per-project settings.json/env/MCP-config changes plus deferring MCP tools, capping thinking, and offloading verbose work to cheap Haiku subagents.

actionable_knobs.name: model (default model per project)
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json (project) or ~/.claude/settings.json (user) — key "model", e.g. {"model": "sonnet"}. Also opusplan for Opus-plan/Sonnet-exec hybrid; /model <alias> persists to ~/.claude/settings.json on Enter; ANTHROPIC_MODEL=sonnet env for one session.
actionable_knobs.saves: Avoids defaulting to Opus 4.8 (5x Haiku, 1.67x Sonnet input) on routine coding; opusplan runs Sonnet for the token-heavy execution phase while keeping Opus only for planning.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Sonnet handles most coding tasks well and costs less than Opus. Reserve Opus for complex architectural decisions." / "opusplan — uses opus during plan mode, then switches to sonnet for execution." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs, /model-config

actionable_knobs.name: effortLevel (adaptive reasoning depth)
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json — {"effortLevel": "medium"} (low/medium/high/xhigh); env CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL=low (highest precedence); /effort command. Subagent frontmatter: effort: low.
actionable_knobs.saves: Thinking tokens bill as output; default high budget can be tens of thousands of tokens/request. low/medium cut thinking output on straightforward tasks.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "medium: Reduces token usage for cost-sensitive work" / "Thinking tokens are billed as output tokens, and the default budget can be tens of thousands of tokens per request." — code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config, /costs

actionable_knobs.name: alwaysThinkingEnabled
actionable_knobs.config_location: ~/.claude/settings.json — {"alwaysThinkingEnabled": false}; or /config toggle thinking mode.
actionable_knobs.saves: Disables extended thinking by default, eliminating tens-of-thousands-of-token thinking output (billed as output) on sessions that don't need deep reasoning.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "alwaysThinkingEnabled: Extended thinking default. Disabled = lower cost." — code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

actionable_knobs.name: MAX_THINKING_TOKENS (fixed-budget models)
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"MAX_THINKING_TOKENS": "8000"}}. Set 0 to disable thinking (needs CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING=1 on Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6; ignored by adaptive-reasoning models and Fable 5).
actionable_knobs.saves: Caps runaway thinking-token spend on fixed-budget models.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "lowering the budget with MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=8000. Adaptive-reasoning models ignore nonzero budgets, so use effort levels there instead." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs

actionable_knobs.name: CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL": "haiku"}}; set "inherit" to restore normal resolution.
actionable_knobs.saves: Forces all subagents/agent-team members onto a cheap model regardless of frontmatter; agent teams use ~7x tokens vs standard sessions, each with its own context window.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "The model to use for all subagents and agent teams. Overrides the per-invocation model parameter and the subagent definition's model frontmatter." — code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

actionable_knobs.name: subagent model frontmatter
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/agents/<name>.md or ~/.claude/agents/<name>.md — YAML key model: haiku (defaults to inherit).
actionable_knobs.saves: Routes read-only scanning/log-parsing subagents to Haiku (~10-20x cheaper/token) instead of inheriting an Opus/Sonnet main session.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "For simple subagent tasks, specify model: haiku in your subagent configuration." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs, /sub-agents

actionable_knobs.name: subagent effort / maxTurns frontmatter
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/agents/<name>.md — effort: low and maxTurns: <N>.
actionable_knobs.saves: effort: low cuts thinking output for mechanical subagent work; maxTurns bounds worst-case token accumulation from runaway subagents.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "effort — Effort level when this subagent is active. Overrides the session effort level." / "maxTurns — Maximum number of agentic turns before the subagent stops." — code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents

actionable_knobs.name: fallbackModel
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json — {"fallbackModel": ["claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5"]}; or --fallback-model sonnet,haiku.
actionable_knobs.saves: Routes overloaded-primary turns to a cheaper fallback instead of failing and forcing an expensive full-context retry.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "When the primary model is overloaded... Claude Code can switch to a fallback model instead of failing the request." — code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

actionable_knobs.name: availableModels + enforceAvailableModels (managed)
actionable_knobs.config_location: Managed settings: /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json (macOS) or /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json (Linux) — {"availableModels": ["sonnet","haiku"], "enforceAvailableModels": true} (v2.1.175+).
actionable_knobs.saves: Hard-blocks Opus/Fable selection across users, subagents, ANTHROPIC_MODEL, and the Default picker option fleet-wide.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Enterprise administrators can use availableModels in managed or policy settings to restrict which models users can select." — code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

actionable_knobs.name: ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL / ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL (pin alias versions)
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL": "claude-haiku-4-5", "ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL": "claude-sonnet-4-6"}}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Pins haiku/sonnet aliases (and opusplan execution, subagent haiku resolution) to known cheap versions, preventing silent auto-upgrade to costlier releases.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "To pin to a specific version... set the corresponding environment variable like ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL." — code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

actionable_knobs.name: autoCompactEnabled
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json — {"autoCompactEnabled": true} (DISABLE_AUTO_COMPACT=1 to disable).
actionable_knobs.saves: Summarizes history near the context limit, keeping per-turn input token counts lower across long sessions.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "autoCompactEnabled: Auto-compact on context limit. Enabled = prevents resets." — code.claude.com/docs/en/settings

actionable_knobs.name: CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW": "50000"}}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Sets a smaller effective window than the model's full window so compaction triggers earlier, keeping average context small.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Context capacity in tokens for auto-compaction calculations... Use lower values to trigger compaction earlier." — code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

actionable_knobs.name: CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE": "70"}} (1-100); applies to subagents too.
actionable_knobs.saves: Triggers compaction at a lower fill percentage, proportionally reducing average per-turn input spend.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Percentage (1-100) of auto-compaction window at which compaction triggers. Lower values compact earlier." — code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

actionable_knobs.name: Compaction instructions in CLAUDE.md
actionable_knobs.config_location: CLAUDE.md (project root or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) — a "# Compact instructions" section, e.g. focus on test output and code changes.
actionable_knobs.saves: Makes every auto-compaction keep high-value tokens and discard low-value history, yielding a smaller post-compact context.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "You can also customize compaction behavior in your CLAUDE.md: # Compact instructions..." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs

actionable_knobs.name: ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H": "1"}} (API key/Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry; subscription plans get 1h automatically).
actionable_knobs.saves: Keeps the cached prefix warm through 5-60 min pauses so resumes hit cache reads (0.1x) instead of full reprocessing (1x). Worth it only if the prefix is read more than ~2.2x/hour given the 2x write premium.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "the TTL stays at the cheaper five minutes by default. To opt into the one-hour TTL, set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1." — code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching

actionable_knobs.name: FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json or shell: FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M=1.
actionable_knobs.saves: Forces the cheaper 5-minute TTL on plans defaulting to 1h, avoiding the 2x cache-write premium for short sessions that never exercise the 1h window.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Set FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M=1 to force the five-minute TTL regardless of authentication." — code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching

actionable_knobs.name: CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER=0
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER": "0"}}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Stabilizes the system-prompt prefix across sessions behind an LLM gateway, raising cache-hit rates so input is reused at ~0.1x instead of reprocessed at 1x.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Set to 0 to omit attribution block from system prompt, improving prompt-cache hit rates with LLM gateways." — code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

actionable_knobs.name: DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER (control when upgrades apply)
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json or shell: DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1; start a fresh session after upgrading rather than resuming a long one.
actionable_knobs.saves: Prevents a background Claude Code upgrade from changing the system prompt and forcing a full uncached reprocess of long resumed histories.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Resuming a session after an upgrade reprocesses the entire conversation history with no cache hits, since the history now sits behind a different system prompt." — code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching

actionable_knobs.name: CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT=1
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT": "1"}}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Removes 1M-context variants from the picker, preventing accidental usage-credit spend on extended context where it isn't included (Pro; Sonnet on subscription plans).
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Set to 1 to disable 1M context window support. This removes 1M model variants from the model picker." — code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

actionable_knobs.name: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH / MCP deferred tool loading
actionable_knobs.config_location: Default behavior (keep it). To tune: env block in .claude/settings.json {"env": {"ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH": "auto:5"}} (auto = 10% window threshold; true forces deferral on Vertex/proxies; false disables). Avoid alwaysLoad: true in .mcp.json/~/.claude.json unless a tool is needed every turn.
actionable_knobs.saves: Keeps MCP tool schemas (~55K tokens for a 5-server setup) out of the cached prefix until used, cutting per-turn input and avoiding cache invalidation on server connect/disconnect.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Tool search keeps MCP context usage low by deferring tool definitions until Claude needs them." / ~55K tokens before conversation for a 5-server setup — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

actionable_knobs.name: MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS
actionable_knobs.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS": "10000"}} (default 25000; warns at 10000).
actionable_knobs.saves: Caps how many tokens any single MCP tool response can occupy; overflow is persisted to disk and replaced with a file reference.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Default limit: 25,000 tokens... To increase the limit: export MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS=50000" (lowering applies the same cap) — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

actionable_knobs.name: disableClaudeAiConnectors / deniedMcpServers / claude mcp remove
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json {"disableClaudeAiConnectors": true} (or env ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false); managed/settings {"deniedMcpServers": [{"serverName": "..."}]}; CLI claude mcp remove <name> / remove from .mcp.json or ~/.claude.json.
actionable_knobs.saves: Removes unused connectors/servers whose tool names + 2KB server instructions load at session start on every turn.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "To disable claude.ai MCP servers... set disableClaudeAiConnectors to true." / "Disable unused servers: Run /mcp..." — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp, /costs

actionable_knobs.name: Subagent mcpServers / tools / disallowedTools frontmatter
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/agents/<name>.md — define an MCP server inline under mcpServers (keeps schemas out of main context), and allowlist with tools: or denylist with disallowedTools:.
actionable_knobs.saves: Confines MCP tool schemas (hundreds of tokens each) to a subagent's run so the main conversation never carries them; tool restriction trims per-turn tool-definition overhead.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "To keep an MCP server out of the main conversation entirely... define it inline here rather than in .mcp.json." — code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents

actionable_knobs.name: claudeMdExcludes
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json (or settings.json) — {"claudeMdExcludes": ["**/vendor/**/CLAUDE.md", "**/node_modules/**/CLAUDE.md"]}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Stops irrelevant ancestor/vendor CLAUDE.md files from concatenating into context at launch, cutting input tokens every turn.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "claudeMdExcludes: Glob patterns for CLAUDE.md files to exclude from loading." — code.claude.com/docs/en/settings, /memory

actionable_knobs.name: Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines; move workflows to skills/path-scoped rules
actionable_knobs.config_location: CLAUDE.md (project/user); move workflow instructions to .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (load on /invoke) or .claude/rules/<topic>.md with YAML paths: globs (load only when matching files are touched). Use <!-- --> for maintainer notes (stripped).
actionable_knobs.saves: CLAUDE.md loads every turn; skills/path-scoped rules load only on demand, shrinking base per-turn context.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Aim to keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines... Skills load on-demand only when invoked." / "conditional rules only apply when Claude is working with files matching the specified patterns." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs, /memory

actionable_knobs.name: outputStyle (avoid Explanatory/Learning)
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json — {"outputStyle": "Default"} (set at session start, not mid-session); or /config > Output style.
actionable_knobs.saves: Default style is most token-efficient; Explanatory/Learning add Insights/TODO sections that inflate output tokens every response.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "The built-in Explanatory and Learning styles produce longer responses than Default by design, which increases output tokens." — code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles

actionable_knobs.name: PreToolUse / PostToolUse hooks (output filtering, blocking, auto-format)
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json hooks.PreToolUse (matcher "Bash", optional if: "Bash(git *)" filter) to grep/trim verbose output and block destructive/protected-file ops with exit 2 or permissionDecision "deny"; hooks.PostToolUse (matcher "Edit|Write") to auto-run a formatter.
actionable_knobs.saves: Filters 10k-line logs/test output down to hundreds of tokens before they enter context; deterministic formatting and blocked-op feedback remove correction/retry round-trips.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "a hook can grep for ERROR and return only matching lines, reducing context from tens of thousands of tokens to hundreds." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs, /hooks-guide

actionable_knobs.name: SessionStart (compact) hook to re-inject context
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json hooks.SessionStart with matcher "compact"; command writes critical project facts to stdout.
actionable_knobs.saves: Re-supplies conventions after compaction so Claude doesn't make wrong assumptions that require wasted correction turns.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Use a SessionStart hook with a compact matcher to re-inject critical context after every compaction." — code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

actionable_knobs.name: Stop hook (verify completion) with 8-block guard
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json hooks.Stop type "prompt" (model defaults to Haiku); script checks stop_hook_active and exits 0 if true; cap raised via CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP.
actionable_knobs.saves: Prevents premature stops that force the user to re-prompt (a new turn + context re-establishment), while the guard prevents unbounded re-trigger loops.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "If the model returns 'ok: false', Claude keeps working... Claude Code overrides a Stop hook after it blocks 8 times in a row." — code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide

actionable_knobs.name: disableAllHooks / CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS
actionable_knobs.config_location: .claude/settings.json {"disableAllHooks": true}; env block {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS": "1"}}.
actionable_knobs.saves: Eliminates background API token consumption from hooks and auto-spawned background subagents (each with its own context window) when not needed.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "Set to 1 to disable all background task functionality..." / disableAllHooks key — code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars, /settings

actionable_knobs.name: Workspace spend & rate limits
actionable_knobs.config_location: Claude Console (platform.claude.com) workspace Limits page for spend/rate caps; /usage-credits inside Claude Code for Pro/Max monthly spend cap.
actionable_knobs.saves: Hard cap on total spend and per-user TPM/RPM, preventing runaway cost from automation or long agent sessions.
actionable_knobs.evidence: "you can set workspace spend limits on the total Claude Code workspace spend." / "set a monthly spend limit on usage credits with the /usage-credits command." — code.claude.com/docs/en/costs

excluded_advisory: Default model tier awareness (subscription plan determines default) — informational, no config to apply
/usage command — visibility only, not a reducer
/clear between tasks — interactive runtime action, not per-project config
/compact with custom instructions — interactive runtime action (the persistable form, Compact instructions in CLAUDE.md, is kept)
MCP tool deferral as a fact / 'enable MCP tool search' duplicates — kept once as ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH/deferred loading knob
/btw for side questions — interactive runtime action
/rewind partial/abandon-path — interactive runtime action, not config
Plan mode before implementation (Shift+Tab) — interactive runtime behavior
Prompt caching (automatic) — default-on, nothing to apply
Delegate verbose operations to subagents (behavioral) — kept only as the configurable subagent frontmatter/mcpServers knobs; the pure behavioral 'use a subagent' tip is advisory
Prefer CLI tools over MCP servers — behavioral preference, no config key
Code intelligence plugins — install/behavioral, not a per-project config optimization scanner can emit
Built-in Explore subagent (Haiku, read-only) — default-on behavior (its deny knob is niche)
Fork subagent prompt-cache sharing (/fork) — interactive runtime action
CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_DISABLE_BUILTIN_AGENTS / suppress per-machine system prompt — Agent-SDK/headless only, not a per-project Claude Code session config
Nested subagent depth cap (max 5) — fixed platform guardrail, not configurable
Subagent memory / skills preload frontmatter — workflow-optimization tips, not waste-driven config a log scanner reliably flags
Minimum cacheable token threshold — informational; no Claude Code config knob

Explicit cache_control breakpoints / automatic top-level caching / pre-warm max_tokens=0 / place breakpoints on static prefix — raw Anthropic API request-body techniques, not Claude Code per-project config
Disable prompt caching per model — diagnostic only, not a cost saver
Keep MCP server instructions concise (2KB) — server-side authoring concern, not client config
anthropic/alwaysLoad and anthropic/maxResultSizeChars per-tool _meta — server-side MCP authoring, not client-side per-project config

Keep model/effort fixed for session; pick at session start; don't change output style mid-session — behavioral session discipline, not a config key (the underlying settings keys model/effortLevel/outputStyle are kept)
Status line context/cache display & /statusline — observability, not a reducer
Agent team size/spawn discipline & CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS — behavioral; cost control kept via CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL/maxTurns
Auto memory (autoMemoryEnabled) — default-on behavior, minor

detection_signals.signal: Expensive model used for trivial/short work
detection_signals.how_to_measure: Per assistant line, read message.model and message.usage. Flag turns where message.model starts with claude-opus or claude-fable/claude-mythos AND output_tokens is small (e.g. < 600) AND no large tool result preceded it. Compute share = (such turns' input+output cost at that model) / total session cost; flag if Opus/Fable turns with output_tokens<600 exceed ~40% of assistant turns or ~50% of spend.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: model (default model per project) / opusplan; effortLevel; availableModels+enforceAvailableModels
detection_signals.severity_hint: high — Opus input is 5x Haiku / 1.67x Sonnet; Fable 10x Haiku. Routine work on Opus/Fable is the single largest avoidable multiplier.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: Sum cache_read_input_tokens and cache_creation_input_tokens across assistant lines. cache_hit_ratio = read / (read + creation). Also compute creation-heavy turns: count turns where cache_creation_input_tokens > cache_read_input_tokens after turn 1. Flag if session ratio < ~0.5 or if creation stays high turn-after-turn (indicates a prefix-invalidating action such as a model/effort switch — detectable as message.model or service_tier changing between consecutive assistant lines in the same sessionId).
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER=0; ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H (for pause-driven misses); DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER (version change mid-session, detectable via the version field changing); model/effortLevel kept fixed
detection_signals.severity_hint: high — a cache miss reprocesses the full context at 1x instead of 0.1x; one miss on a long session can cost as much as the whole prior conversation.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: From cache_creation.ephemeral_1h_input_tokens vs ephemeral_5m_input_tokens: count 1h cache-write tokens, then check whether they are subsequently read. If sum(ephemeral_1h_input_tokens) is large but the number of cache_read turns within the following hour (by timestamp) is low (e.g. < ~2 reads per 1h write block), the 2x write premium is wasted.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M=1 (when sessions are short / few reads); conversely ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H only when reads/hour are high
detection_signals.severity_hint: medium — 1h writes cost 2x base input vs 1.25x for 5m; net loss only when the prefix is read fewer than ~2.2 times before expiry.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: Detect MCP usage via attributionMcpServer / attributionMcpTool fields and count distinct servers. Proxy for upfront schema cost: high first-turn input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens on the session's first assistant line that scales with number of distinct attributionMcpServer values. Flag if many MCP servers appear but only a small subset of attributionMcpTool values are ever actually invoked (configured-but-unused servers), or if first-turn cached prefix is unusually large.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH (keep deferred / auto:N); disableClaudeAiConnectors / deniedMcpServers / claude mcp remove; subagent mcpServers frontmatter; avoid alwaysLoad
detection_signals.severity_hint: high — a 5-server setup can add ~55K tokens to every turn's prefix; deferral cuts this to near zero for unused schemas.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: Track non-cached input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens per assistant turn over the session. Flag if per-turn total context climbs monotonically past a threshold (e.g. > 120K) without any compaction event, or if baseline first-turn input (system prompt + CLAUDE.md) is high (e.g. > 15K before any user content) — indicating large CLAUDE.md / many undeferred tools.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: autoCompactEnabled; CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW; CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE; Compaction instructions in CLAUDE.md; claudeMdExcludes; keep CLAUDE.md <200 lines / move to skills/rules; outputStyle Default
detection_signals.severity_hint: high — input tokens are re-sent every turn; an unbounded context multiplies cost across the whole session.

detection_signals.signal: Heavy verbose work done on main thread instead of a cheap subagent
detection_signals.how_to_measure: Identify main-thread turns (isSidechain absent/false) with very large input_tokens driven by tool results (large toolUseResult on the preceding user line, e.g. test/log/file dumps) running on an expensive message.model. Compare against isSidechain=true turns to see if subagents are underused. Flag main-thread turns where a >10K-token tool result is processed on Opus/Sonnet with low resulting output_tokens.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=haiku; subagent model/effort/maxTurns/tools frontmatter; PreToolUse output-filtering hooks; MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS
detection_signals.severity_hint: high — verbose output processed on the main thread both inflates that turn and persists in context for all later turns at full input rate.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: Hash identical tool invocations (same tool name + same toolUseResult/input signature) within a sessionId; count exact repeats. Also detect Stop/retry loops via repeated near-identical assistant turns and stop_hook_active patterns. Flag sessions where >~15% of tool calls are exact duplicates or where the same file/command is re-read 3+ times.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks (auto-format, deny-with-reason feedback); Stop hook with completion check + 8-block guard; subagent memory to avoid re-exploration
detection_signals.severity_hint: medium — each redundant call is an extra turn re-sending the full context; loops can accumulate unbounded tokens.

detection_signals.how_to_measure: Sum message.usage.server_tool_use.web_search_requests and web_fetch_requests across assistant lines per session. Flag sessions with high counts (e.g. > ~20 web_search_requests) or fetches returning very large bodies that spike the next turn's input_tokens.
detection_signals.maps_to_knob: PreToolUse output-filtering hooks (trim fetched bodies); delegate fetching to a Haiku subagent (CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL / subagent model frontmatter) to keep large fetched content out of main context; MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS for MCP-based fetch tools
detection_signals.severity_hint: medium — web_search/web_fetch are billed server-tool requests and the returned content inflates subsequent main-thread input if not isolated or trimmed.

research_knobs_actionable.name: model (settings key)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Sets the default model for all sessions. Use cheaper/faster model aliases (haiku, sonnet) or specific IDs like claude-haiku-4-5 instead of opus or fable to reduce per-token costs directly. The haiku alias resolves to the fast, low-cost Haiku model and is specifically recommended for simple tasks.

research_knobs_actionable.name: effortLevel (settings key)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Controls adaptive reasoning depth. Lower effort levels (low, medium) cause the model to think less per step, spending fewer output tokens on reasoning. Default is 'high' on most models. 'low' is for short, scoped, latency-sensitive tasks; 'medium' reduces token usage for cost-sensitive work.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json — key: "effortLevel", e.g. {"effortLevel": "low"}. Also settable via env var CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL=low or CLI flag --effort low or /effort command.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Thinking tokens are billed as output tokens. 'The default budget can be tens of thousands of tokens per request depending on the model.' Lower effort = fewer thinking tokens per turn.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "medium: Reduces token usage for cost-sensitive work that can trade off some intelligence" and "low: Reserve for short, scoped, latency-sensitive tasks" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

research_knobs_actionable.name: fallbackModel (settings key)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Defines an ordered fallback chain (up to 3 models) when the primary model is overloaded or unavailable. Instead of failing and requiring a retry (which wastes a full context-window worth of tokens re-sent), Claude Code automatically falls back to a cheaper/faster model for that turn only.

research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids failed requests and expensive retries by routing overloaded-model turns to a cheaper fallback automatically.

research_knobs_actionable.name: autoCompactEnabled (settings key)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: When enabled, Claude Code automatically summarizes conversation history when approaching the context limit instead of letting the context fill up and requiring a full reset. Compaction replaces accumulated turns with a dense summary, reducing context size and therefore per-turn input token costs going forward.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json or ~/.claude/settings.json — key: "autoCompactEnabled": true
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents the context window from bloating with stale turns, keeping per-message input token counts lower across long sessions.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "autoCompactEnabled: Auto-compact on context limit. Enabled = prevents resets" and Claude Code "automatically optimizes costs through... auto-compaction, which summarizes conversation history when approaching context limits." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs

research_knobs_actionable.name: CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW (env var)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Sets the effective context capacity (in tokens) used for auto-compaction calculations. Setting a lower value than the model's actual window triggers compaction earlier, keeping context smaller and reducing per-turn input token costs before the window is fully saturated.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW": "50000"}} or system environment variable
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Forces earlier compaction, preventing context from growing to expensive full-window sizes between automatic compactions.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW: Context capacity in tokens for auto-compaction calculations. Defaults to model's full window (200K or 1M). Use lower values to trigger compaction earlier." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Opts API-key and third-party-provider sessions into the one-hour prompt cache TTL (default is 5 minutes for API users). Longer TTL means the cached prefix survives longer breaks between messages, so returning after a pause still hits the cache instead of reprocessing the full context.

research_knobs_actionable.saves: Reduces cache-miss turns for users who pause between prompts. Cache read tokens are billed at ~10% of standard input rate vs 100% on a miss.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "On an API key, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry... the TTL stays at the cheaper five minutes by default. To opt into the one-hour TTL, set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching

research_knobs_actionable.name: CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER (env var, set to 0)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Omits the attribution block from the system prompt. The attribution block changes per-session, which causes system-prompt cache misses when routing through an LLM gateway where multiple Claude Code sessions share cache infrastructure. Removing it stabilizes the system prompt prefix and improves cache hit rates.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: env block in .claude/settings.json: {"env": {"CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER": "0"}} or system environment variable
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Improves prompt-cache hit rates on LLM gateway deployments by making the system prompt identical across sessions, so cached input tokens are reused at ~10% billing rate instead of being reprocessed at full rate.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER: Set to 0 to omit attribution block from system prompt, improving prompt-cache hit rates with LLM gateways." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars

research_knobs_actionable.name: MCP deferred tool loading (default behavior, configurable per server with alwaysLoad)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: By default on supported models, MCP tool definitions are deferred — only tool names enter the system prompt context, and full definitions are fetched on demand via tool search when Claude actually uses a tool. This keeps the system prompt prefix smaller and avoids cache invalidation when MCP servers connect/disconnect.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .mcp.json or ~/.claude.json MCP server definitions — avoid setting alwaysLoad: true unless a server must be fully loaded upfront. MCP tool search is on by default on supported models.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: MCP tool definitions can be thousands of tokens each. Deferring them keeps the system prompt short, reducing per-turn input costs and preventing cache invalidations when servers change.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "MCP tool definitions are deferred by default, so only tool names enter context until Claude uses a specific tool." and "Deferred tools... a server connecting, disconnecting, or changing its tool list only appends new content and doesn't disturb anything already cached." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching

research_knobs_actionable.name: model field in settings.json (per-project or user-level)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Sets the default model for every session that starts in that project (or globally). Project-level .claude/settings.json overrides the user default; managed/policy settings override both. By pinning a cheaper alias such as 'sonnet' or 'haiku', every session avoids starting on Opus 4.8 (the default for Max/Team/Enterprise pay-as-you-go), eliminating unnecessary Opus spend on routine coding tasks.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json (project) or ~/.claude/settings.json (user), key: "model", e.g. {"model": "sonnet"}
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents defaulting to Opus 4.8 on high-tier plans for every session; Sonnet costs ~10x less per token than Opus.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Configure permanently in your settings file using the model field." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config. Default for Max/Team Premium/Enterprise pay-as-you-go is Opus 4.8; for Pro/Team Standard it is Sonnet 4.6.

research_knobs_actionable.name: /model command (interactive model switch)

Allows dropping to haiku or sonnet instantly without restarting.

research_knobs_actionable.name: CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT env var

research_knobs_actionable.name: subagent `model` frontmatter field
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Each subagent .md file can declare a `model` field (alias or full model ID). Setting it to `haiku` routes that subagent's work to the cheapest/fastest model, leaving the main thread on a more capable model. Without this field it defaults to `inherit` (same model as main conversation).
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/agents/<name>.md or ~/.claude/agents/<name>.md — YAML frontmatter key `model`, e.g. `model: haiku`
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Removes per-subagent cost inflation: a Haiku subagent doing read-only scanning or log parsing costs ~10-20x less per token than the same work in an Opus/Sonnet main session.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Control costs by routing tasks to faster, cheaper models like Haiku" and "For simple subagent tasks, specify model: haiku in your subagent configuration" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code subagents and the Task/Agent tool — offloading context to subagents and assigning cheaper models to reduce main-thread token spend

research_knobs_actionable.name: CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL environment variable
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Global override that forces ALL subagents and agent-team members to use a specified model, regardless of per-subagent `model` frontmatter or the per-invocation `model` parameter. Overrides every other model-resolution step for subagents. Set to `inherit` to re-enable normal resolution.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=<alias|full-model-id>`, e.g. `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=haiku`. Can also be placed in settings.json under the `env` key.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Pins every spawned subagent to a cheap model fleet-wide, preventing any subagent from accidentally running on Opus when only Haiku-class reasoning is needed.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "The model to use for all subagents and agent teams. Overrides the per-invocation model parameter and the subagent definition's model frontmatter. Set to inherit to use normal model resolution instead" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#environment-variables
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code subagents and the Task/Agent tool — offloading context to subagents and assigning cheaper models to reduce main-thread token spend

research_knobs_actionable.name: Built-in Explore subagent (Haiku, read-only, skips CLAUDE.md + git status)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: The built-in Explore subagent runs on Haiku, restricts tool access to read-only operations, and deliberately skips loading CLAUDE.md files and the parent session git status. research_knobs_actionable.name: subagent `maxTurns` frontmatter field
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Caps the number of agentic turns (tool calls + responses) a subagent can execute before stopping. Without a cap, runaway subagents can accumulate large token counts. Setting a tight `maxTurns` appropriate for the task bounds worst-case subagent cost.

research_knobs_actionable.name: Fork subagent prompt-cache sharing with parent
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A fork subagent (`/fork <directive>` or spawned via CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1) inherits the parent conversation's system prompt and tool definitions verbatim, which means its first request reuses the parent's existing prompt cache. No cache warm-up cost is paid. A fresh named subagent has a separate cache.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Slash command `/fork <directive>` in interactive mode. Or env var `CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1` to let Claude spawn forks automatically. Requires Claude Code v2.1.117+; /fork enabled by default from v2.1.161+.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates the cache warm-up cost for side tasks that need the same system prompt and tools as the main session, reducing the effective cost of that subagent's first request.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Because a fork's system prompt and tool definitions are identical to the parent, its first request reuses the parent's prompt cache. This makes forking cheaper than spawning a fresh subagent for tasks that need the same context" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#how-forks-differ-from-named-subagents

research_knobs_actionable.name: Subagent auto-compaction via CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Subagents support the same auto-compaction logic as the main conversation. Setting CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE to a lower percentage (e.g. 50) causes compaction to trigger earlier in a subagent's run, summarizing history before the context grows very large. This keeps per-turn costs lower for long-running subagents.

research_knobs_actionable.saves: Reduces the token payload for each subsequent turn in a long-running subagent by triggering compaction before the context window fills, replacing detailed history with a compact summary.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Subagents support automatic compaction using the same logic as the main conversation. Compaction triggers under the same conditions, and CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE applies to subagents as well" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#auto-compaction

research_knobs_actionable.name: Enable 1-hour cache TTL (API key / cloud provider)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Sets cache TTL from 5 minutes to 1 hour via the env var ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1. Cache writes cost 2x base input price instead of 1.25x, but cache reads remain at 0.1x. Pays off if the same prefix is read more than ~2.2 times per hour. On Claude subscription plans, 1h TTL is applied automatically at no extra cost.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1, set in .claude/settings.json under the env block for org-wide policy, or in shell environment for per-session use
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates full-prefix reprocessing for sessions with pauses longer than 5 minutes but shorter than 1 hour; keeps the cache warm through coffee-break-length interruptions without re-paying cache write costs
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "On an API key, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, or Claude Platform on AWS, you pay the per-token rates, so the TTL stays at the cheaper five minutes by default. To opt into the one-hour TTL, set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Force 5-minute cache TTL (override subscription default)

research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable FORCE_PROMPT_CACHING_5M=1, set in shell environment or .claude/settings.json env block
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids paying the 2x cache write premium (1-hour TTL rate) when sessions are short and the 1h TTL would never be exercised

Primary use is debugging unexpected cache behaviour rather than cost reduction.

research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Explicit cache_control breakpoints on static content blocks
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Add cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'} to the last static block in the system prompt and/or messages. The API caches all content up to that breakpoint and replays it at 0.1x base cost on subsequent requests. Up to 4 explicit breakpoints per request. For 1h TTL, add ttl: '1h' inside cache_control.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: API request body — cache_control field on system content blocks or messages content blocks; e.g. {"type": "text", "text": "...", "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}} or {"type": "ephemeral", "ttl": "1h"}
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Cache reads billed at 10% of standard input token price (0.1x multiplier). A single cache hit on a 10k-token system prompt saves 90% of that prefix's cost; break-even vs 5m write is 1 hit; break-even vs 1h write is ~2 hits.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: Pricing table: "5-minute cache writes: 1.25x base input price; 1-hour cache writes: 2.0x base input price; Cache reads/refreshes: 0.1x base input price" and breakpoint syntax — https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Automatic (top-level) caching via single cache_control on request
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Place a single cache_control field at the top level of the request object (not inside a specific block). Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, and Microsoft Foundry automatically cache the entire prefix. Not supported on Bedrock or Vertex AI — those require explicit block-level breakpoints.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: API request body top-level field: {"cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}} or with TTL: {"cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral", "ttl": "1h"}}
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Same 90% read-cost saving as explicit breakpoints, with zero per-block annotation overhead. Only available on Claude API and select platforms.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Automatic caching is available on the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, and Microsoft Foundry (beta). Bedrock and Vertex AI do not support automatic caching." — https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Place cache breakpoints on static prefix, never on dynamic content
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: The cache key is an exact prefix hash. Any token that changes between requests (timestamps, user IDs, per-request data) must appear AFTER the last cache breakpoint. Static system prompt and tool definitions should be at the front, marked with cache_control; dynamic user message appended after. Common mistake: marking a block that contains a timestamp, which produces a unique hash on every request and a permanent cache miss.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: API request body structure — ordering of content blocks in system array and messages array
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents paying 1.25x or 2x cache write cost on every request with zero reads, which would be worse than no caching. Correct placement enables consistent 0.1x read cost on all subsequent requests.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Place cache_control on the last block whose prefix is identical across requests." and "This fails because the timestamp changes on each request, making the prefix hash different every time." — https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Pre-warm cache with max_tokens=0 request
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Send a request with max_tokens=0 before actual user traffic begins. This writes the system prompt and tool definitions to cache and returns immediately with empty content and stop_reason: 'max_tokens'. Subsequent real requests then hit the cache on the first user turn. Incurs cache write charge but zero output tokens.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: API request body parameter: max_tokens=0 combined with cache_control on system blocks. Cannot be used with stream:true, extended thinking, structured outputs, or tool_choice: 'tool'/'any'.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Ensures the first real user request gets a cache hit instead of paying full input token price to write and process the prefix simultaneously. Critical for high-traffic applications launching simultaneously.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Use max_tokens: 0 to load content into cache before user requests... Incurs cache write charge (check cache_creation_input_tokens). Zero output tokens billed." — https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Keep model and effort level fixed for the session duration
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Each model and each effort level has its own separate cache namespace. Switching /model or /effort mid-session causes the next request to reprocess the entire conversation history with zero cache hits, even if the content is identical. Pick model and effort at session start and hold them.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Claude Code: set default model in /config or settings.json key 'model'; set effort in /effort or settings.json key 'effort'. Avoid the opusplan model setting if cache-efficiency is critical, since it switches between Opus and Sonnet on every plan-mode toggle.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids a full uncached turn that can cost as much as the entire prior conversation compressed into one request; each such miss is billed at full 1x input rates instead of 0.1x.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Each model has its own cache. Switching with /model means the next request reads the entire conversation history with no cache hits... The cache is keyed by effort level as well as model, so switching with /effort means the next request reads the entire conversation history with no cache hits." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Run /compact at natural task boundaries, not mid-task
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Compaction replaces message history with a summary, invalidating the conversation cache layer. The compaction summarisation call itself reads the existing cache cheaply (it shares the prefix), but the first turn after compaction must rebuild the conversation cache from scratch. Running /compact between tasks rather than during one avoids paying this rebuild cost at a moment where the context being discarded is still actively needed.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Claude Code interactive command: /compact [focus hint], e.g. /compact Focus on code changes and test output. Compaction instructions can also be set in CLAUDE.md under a '# Compact instructions' heading.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents wasted cache rebuild work mid-task; the post-compaction conversation is shorter and its cache rebuild is proportionally cheaper. Also reduces all subsequent per-turn input costs because the context window is smaller.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "To choose when its overhead happens, run /compact at a natural break in your work, such as between tasks, instead of waiting for auto-compaction to trigger mid-task." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use /rewind instead of /compact to abandon a failed path
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: /rewind truncates the conversation back to an earlier turn. The remaining history is identical to what the cache was built from at that turn, so the next request reads the earlier cache entry (which was kept warm by subsequent turns that passed through it). No cache rebuild occurs, unlike /compact.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Claude Code interactive command: /rewind or double-tap Escape to restore to a previous checkpoint
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids both the compaction overhead AND a full cache rebuild; restores a cheaply-cached prefix rather than creating a new, uncached one.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Rewinding truncates back to a prefix that is already cached, rather than building a new one as compaction does." and "/rewind... the next request hits the earlier cache entry. Every turn since then has read through that prefix, which kept the entry warm." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Keep MCP tool definitions deferred (do not use alwaysLoad)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: By default on supported models, MCP tool definitions are deferred — only tool names enter the system prompt prefix; full definitions load only when Claude uses a specific tool. This means connecting, disconnecting, or updating an MCP server mid-session does NOT invalidate the cached prefix. Marking a server or tool as alwaysLoad or disabling tool search forces definitions into the prefix, so any MCP change causes a full cache miss.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Claude Code MCP server configuration — avoid setting alwaysLoad: true in MCP server config; do not set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to a gateway that disables tool search; tool search is unavailable on Haiku models and Vertex AI by default.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents expensive full-context cache misses triggered by MCP server reconnects, dynamic tool updates, or server config changes that would otherwise cost a full uncached turn.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Deferred tools, the default on supported models: a server connecting, disconnecting, or changing its tool list only appends new content and doesn't disturb anything already cached." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Apply Claude Code upgrades at session start, not mid-session
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A new Claude Code version updates the system prompt or tool definitions, invalidating the entire cache. Auto-update downloads versions in the background but applies on next launch. Setting DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1 lets you control exactly when upgrades apply so they do not land during an active long session.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1 (set in shell profile or .claude/settings.json env block)
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents a surprise full-cache-rebuild turn mid-session caused by a silent background upgrade taking effect. Especially important before resuming long sessions with /resume after a Claude Code update.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Auto-update downloads new versions in the background but applies them on the next launch, never mid-session... Resuming a session after an upgrade reprocesses the entire conversation history with no cache hits, since the history now sits behind a different system prompt." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Suppress per-machine system prompt sections for fleet/Agent SDK use
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Claude Code's system prompt embeds working directory, platform, shell, OS version, and auto-memory paths, making the prefix unique per machine. Agent SDK callers can suppress these per-machine sections so all fleet instances share the same prefix and therefore share the same cache entry. See Agent SDK docs for modifying system prompts.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Agent SDK configuration — see https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/modifying-system-prompts for the suppress-machine-info option
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Converts per-machine cache misses into cross-machine cache hits for automated fleets; instead of N machines each paying 1.25x for a cache write of the same prefix, all N share one write and pay 0.1x on reads.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "For Agent SDK callers running fleets of automated processes, see improve prompt caching across users and machines to suppress the per-machine sections of the system prompt and share the cache across machines." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Monitor cache performance via statusline current_usage fields
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Claude Code exposes cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens in the current_usage object readable by statusline scripts. A high read-to-creation ratio confirms caching is working. If creation stays high turn after turn, a prefix-invalidating action is occurring and can be diagnosed against the known invalidation list.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Claude Code statusline script — read current_usage.cache_creation_input_tokens and current_usage.cache_read_input_tokens; configure via /config statusline or see https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Not a direct cost saving, but enables detection of cache regressions (e.g. unexpected model switch, MCP reconnect) before they accumulate significant cost over a long session.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "A high read-to-creation ratio means caching is working well. If creation stays high turn after turn, something is changing in your prefix." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: Subagent model selection: use Sonnet with 5-minute TTL awareness
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Subagents start with their own cache namespace (no parent cache inheritance) and always use the 5-minute TTL even on subscription plans. Short-lived subagents rarely get a second cache hit within the TTL window, so paying 1.25x for a cache write that yields zero reads is worse than no caching. For costly multi-turn subagent tasks, keep the subagent alive long enough across turns to amortise the cache write.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Subagent configuration: set model: haiku for simple tasks (lowest base price), model: sonnet for general tasks. See subagent spec in .claude/agents/*.md or inline subagent definition.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids paying 1.25x cache write on a subagent that exits after one turn and never reads from cache; choose cheap models to reduce base input cost when caching won't help.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Subagents use the five-minute TTL even on a subscription, since the automatic one-hour TTL applies to the main conversation." and costs page: "Use Sonnet for teammates. It balances capability and cost for coordination tasks." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching, https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Prompt caching in Claude Code and the Anthropic API

research_knobs_actionable.name: PreToolUse hook — block destructive Bash commands
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Fires before a tool call executes and can block it entirely. A script that exits with code 2 cancels the tool call; stderr is fed back to Claude as feedback so it can adjust without looping. Prevents expensive recoveries from destructive operations (e.g., `rm -rf`, `DROP TABLE`).
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json under hooks.PreToolUse with matcher 'Bash'; hook script exits 2 to block, writes reason to stderr
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates token waste from destructive operations that would require expensive recovery turns; prevents Claude from blindly retrying after blocked calls by receiving clear feedback
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Exit 2: the action is blocked. Write a reason to stderr, and Claude receives it as feedback so it can adjust." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code hooks — preventing wasted/looping work, auto-formatting, and blocking expensive operations

research_knobs_actionable.name: PreToolUse hook — block edits to protected files
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A PreToolUse hook with matcher 'Edit|Write' checks the target file path against protected patterns (.env, package-lock.json, .git/) and exits 2 to block. Claude receives the stderr reason and adjusts its approach rather than wasting a turn on a doomed write.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json under hooks.PreToolUse with matcher 'Edit|Write'; reference script at .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents token-consuming edit attempts that would be denied anyway, and avoids follow-on correction turns
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Prevent Claude from modifying sensitive files like .env, package-lock.json, or anything in .git/. Claude receives feedback explaining why the edit was blocked, so it can adjust its approach." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code hooks — preventing wasted/looping work, auto-formatting, and blocking expensive operations

research_knobs_actionable.name: PostToolUse hook — auto-format code after edits (Prettier)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Runs Prettier (or any formatter) deterministically on every file Claude writes/edits. Because formatting is guaranteed by the hook, Claude never needs to re-read, re-check, or re-attempt formatting in a follow-up turn, eliminating a common source of extra round-trips.

research_knobs_actionable.name: SessionStart hook with 'compact' matcher — re-inject context after compaction
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Fires when a session resumes after auto-compaction. Any text written to stdout is injected into Claude's context. Re-supplying critical project facts (conventions, current sprint, tool preferences) after compaction prevents Claude from making wrong assumptions that lead to wasted correction turns.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json under hooks.SessionStart with matcher 'compact'; command: any shell command writing reminders to stdout

research_knobs_actionable.name: Stop hook (prompt type) — verify task completion before allowing Claude to stop
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A prompt-based Stop hook calls a Claude model (Haiku by default) to evaluate whether all requested tasks are actually complete. If the model returns {"ok": false, "reason": "..."}, Claude keeps working using the reason as its next instruction. Prevents premature stopping that would require the user to re-prompt.

research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code hooks — preventing wasted/looping work, auto-formatting, and blocking expensive operations

research_knobs_actionable.name: Stop hook — cap at 8 blocks with stop_hook_active guard
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Claude Code automatically overrides a Stop hook after it blocks 8 consecutive times without progress to prevent infinite looping. Hook scripts should check the 'stop_hook_active' field from JSON input and exit 0 when true, to avoid being forcibly overridden. The cap can be raised via CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP env var.

research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Claude Code overrides a Stop hook after it blocks 8 times in a row without progress. Your hook script needs to check whether it already triggered a continuation. Parse the stop_hook_active field from the JSON input and exit early if it's true." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code hooks — preventing wasted/looping work, auto-formatting, and blocking expensive operations

research_knobs_actionable.name: PreToolUse hook with 'if' field — fine-grained argument-level filtering
