research_knobs_actionable.name: MCP Tool Search (deferred tool loading)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Tool schemas are NOT loaded into context at session start. Only tool names and server instructions load upfront. Claude uses a ToolSearch call to discover and load only the schemas it actually needs for the current task. This avoids the ~55K+ token overhead of loading all tools from multiple MCP servers before any conversation begins.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH env var (set in shell or in .claude/settings.json under the 'env' key). Default (unset) = all MCP tools deferred. Set 'true' to force deferral even on Vertex AI / proxies. Set 'auto' for threshold mode (defer if tools exceed 10% of context window). Set 'auto:N' for custom % threshold (e.g., ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:5). Set 'false' to disable and load all upfront.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates the upfront token cost of unused MCP tool schemas. A five-server setup (GitHub 35 tools ~26K tokens, Slack 11 tools ~21K tokens, etc.) consumed ~55K tokens before any conversation; with tool search this drops to near zero for unused schemas. Engineering blog reports 85% reduction in context token usage (191,300 tokens preserved vs. 122,800 with traditional upfront loading).
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Tool search keeps MCP context usage low by deferring tool definitions until Claude needs them. Only tool names and server instructions load at session start, so adding more MCP servers has minimal impact on your context window." — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp. Numbers: "approximately 55K tokens before the conversation even starts" for 5-server setup — anthropic.com/engineering/advanced-tool-use
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: alwaysLoad per-server flag (exempt specific servers from deferral)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: By default with tool search enabled, all MCP servers are deferred. Setting alwaysLoad: true on a server forces its tools to load into context at session start. This is the inverse lever — use it only for a small number of tools Claude needs on every turn, as each upfront tool consumes context. All other servers remain deferred.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .mcp.json (project root) or ~/.claude.json (user scope) — add 'alwaysLoad': true to the server's config object. Example: {"mcpServers": {"core-tools": {"type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.example.com/mcp", "alwaysLoad": true}}}. Requires Claude Code v2.1.121+.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Lets you opt-in only the handful of truly always-needed tools, leaving everything else deferred. Prevents per-turn ToolSearch overhead for tools that would otherwise be searched every time.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "If a server's tools should always be visible to Claude without a search step, set alwaysLoad to true... Use this for a small number of tools that Claude needs on every turn, since each upfront tool consumes context that would otherwise be available for your conversation." — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: anthropic/alwaysLoad per-tool meta annotation
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: An MCP server author can mark individual tools (not the whole server) as always-loaded by including '"anthropic/alwaysLoad": true' in the tool's _meta object in the tools/list response. This loads only that specific tool upfront while keeping the rest of the server's tools deferred.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Set server-side in the MCP server's tools/list response: {"name": "my_tool", "_meta": {"anthropic/alwaysLoad": true}}. No client-side config file change needed.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Fine-grained control: single high-frequency tools get upfront context access without loading the entire server's schema set.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "An MCP server can also mark individual tools as always-loaded by including 'anthropic/alwaysLoad': true in the tool's _meta object, which has the same effect for that tool only." — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS — limit MCP tool output size
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: MCP tool responses can be arbitrarily large and consume context tokens. Claude Code warns at 10,000-token output and truncates at 25,000 tokens by default. Lowering MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS caps how many tokens any single MCP tool response can occupy in context. Large outputs exceeding the threshold are persisted to disk and replaced with a file reference.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS (default: 25000). Set in shell: export MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS=10000 or in .claude/settings.json under the 'env' key: {"env": {"MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS": "10000"}}. Warning threshold is fixed at 10,000 tokens.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents runaway context consumption from MCP servers that return large datasets, logs, or reports. Each token over the cap is truncated, keeping context available for the actual task.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Claude Code displays a warning when any MCP tool output exceeds 10,000 tokens... Default limit: 25,000 tokens... To increase the limit: export MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS=50000" — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp (same mechanism applies to lowering the limit)
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: anthropic/maxResultSizeChars per-tool annotation (server-side)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: MCP server authors can annotate individual tools with a maximum result size in characters via _meta["anthropic/maxResultSizeChars"]. Claude Code raises (or lowers) that tool's truncation threshold to the annotated value, up to a hard ceiling of 500,000 characters. Results exceeding this for text are persisted to disk and replaced with a file reference, keeping them out of the live context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Set server-side in tools/list response: {"name": "get_schema", "_meta": {"anthropic/maxResultSizeChars": 200000}}. No client configuration required; takes precedence over MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS for text content.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents specific high-output tools (e.g., schema dumps, file trees) from blowing up context without requiring users to globally raise MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Claude Code raises that tool's threshold to the annotated value, up to a hard ceiling of 500,000 characters... Without the annotation, results that exceed the default threshold are persisted to disk and replaced with a file reference" — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: disableClaudeAiConnectors — disable cloud connectors entirely
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Claude.ai connectors (provisioned from your claude.ai account, e.g., Microsoft 365, Gmail, Slack, etc.) are loaded automatically when authenticated via Claude.ai subscription. Each connector adds tool schemas to context. Setting disableClaudeAiConnectors: true prevents all of these from loading.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json (project level): {"disableClaudeAiConnectors": true}. Or env var: ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false. Also settable at user scope in ~/.claude/settings.json. Uses any-source-true semantics: true in ANY settings source wins.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates all context tokens from cloud connector tool schemas for projects that don't need them. Particularly useful for isolated or security-sensitive projects.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "To disable claude.ai MCP servers in Claude Code, set disableClaudeAiConnectors to true in any settings scope... You can also set the ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS environment variable to false" — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: deniedMcpServers — block specific MCP servers by name or URL
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Instead of disabling all connectors, you can block individual MCP servers by name or URL pattern using deniedMcpServers. Blocked servers do not connect and their tool schemas never enter context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Managed settings file (managed-settings.json in system directory: /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/ on macOS, /etc/claude-code/ on Linux, C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\ on Windows) or .claude/settings.json. Key: 'deniedMcpServers'. Example: {"deniedMcpServers": [{"serverName": "claude.ai Slack"}]}.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Surgically removes token overhead from specific unused servers without affecting other connectors.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "To block individual claude.ai connectors instead of all of them, add them to deniedMcpServers by name or by URL pattern." — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: Remove unused MCP servers via claude mcp remove
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Any MCP server that is configured but not needed for a project will connect at session start and potentially load tool schemas into context (if alwaysLoad or ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false). Removing unused servers via 'claude mcp remove <name>' prevents any connection overhead and eliminates the server's instruction text from context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: CLI command: 'claude mcp remove <server-name>'. For project-scoped servers, remove the entry from .mcp.json. For user-scoped servers, remove from ~/.claude.json. Check current servers with 'claude mcp list'.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Server instructions still load at session start even with tool search enabled (only schemas are deferred, not server instruction text). Removing truly unused servers eliminates even this startup cost.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Only tool names and server instructions load at session start" — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp. 'claude mcp remove github' is the documented command for removal.
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:N threshold mode
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Instead of full deferral or full upfront loading, 'auto' mode loads MCP tool schemas upfront only if they fit within N% of the context window (default 10% with 'auto', custom % with 'auto:N'). Tools that would exceed the threshold are deferred. This is a middle ground that avoids ToolSearch round-trips for small schema sets while deferring large ones.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto (10% threshold) or ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:5 (5% threshold) or ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:N for any N from 0-100. Set in shell or in .claude/settings.json under 'env': {"env": {"ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH": "auto:5"}}.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Balances context savings (defer large schema sets) with latency savings (avoid ToolSearch round-trips for small sets). Tuning N lower forces more deferral and keeps more context free.
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "If you prefer threshold-based loading, set ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto to load schemas upfront when they fit within 10% of the context window and defer only the overflow... auto:N — Threshold mode with a custom percentage, where N is 0-100" — code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code MCP servers and tool definitions — token overhead reduction

research_knobs_actionable.name: Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: CLAUDE.md is loaded into the context window at session start on every turn. Files over 200 lines consume more context tokens and empirically reduce Claude's adherence to the instructions — rules get 'lost in the noise'. Keeping under 200 lines reduces per-session input tokens.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: ./CLAUDE.md or ./.claude/CLAUDE.md — edit by hand or via /memory command
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Reduces per-session input token baseline; improves instruction adherence so fewer correction turns are needed
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "target under 200 lines per CLAUDE.md file. Longer files consume more context and reduce adherence." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Move workflow/task-specific instructions from CLAUDE.md to skills
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: CLAUDE.md is injected on every turn. Skills load on-demand when invoked or when Claude determines they are relevant. Moving specialized workflows (PR reviews, database migrations, etc.) into .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md means those tokens are only present when needed, not on every message.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md — create directory and SKILL.md file; invoke with /<skill-name>
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates context tokens for specialized instructions on every unrelated turn
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "If it contains detailed instructions for specific workflows (like PR reviews or database migrations), those tokens are present even when you're doing unrelated work. Skills load on-demand only when invoked, so moving specialized instructions into skills keeps your base context smaller." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use path-scoped rules in .claude/rules/ instead of CLAUDE.md
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Rules in .claude/rules/ with YAML `paths:` frontmatter only load into context when Claude reads files matching the glob pattern, not on every turn. Rules without paths load at launch like CLAUDE.md, but rules with paths are conditional — saving tokens on turns that don't touch those files.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/rules/<topic>.md — add YAML frontmatter with `paths: ['src/api/**/*.ts']` to scope loading
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates context tokens for file-type-specific rules on every turn that does not involve matching files
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Rules can be scoped to specific files using YAML frontmatter with the `paths` field. These conditional rules only apply when Claude is working with files matching the specified patterns." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use HTML comments in CLAUDE.md for maintainer notes
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Block-level HTML comments (<!-- ... -->) in CLAUDE.md files are stripped before the content is injected into Claude's context window. This lets you leave notes for human maintainers without spending any context tokens on them.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: ./CLAUDE.md — wrap maintainer notes in <!-- --> blocks
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Removes documentation/commentary tokens from context while keeping the file human-readable
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Block-level HTML comments (<!-- maintainer notes -->) in CLAUDE.md files are stripped before the content is injected into Claude's context. Use them to leave notes for human maintainers without spending context tokens on them." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use claudeMdExcludes to skip irrelevant ancestor CLAUDE.md files
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: In a monorepo, CLAUDE.md files from all ancestor directories and other teams are concatenated into context at launch. The claudeMdExcludes setting skips specific files by glob path, removing irrelevant instructions from context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json — set key `claudeMdExcludes` to an array of glob patterns matching absolute file paths
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates tokens from CLAUDE.md files for other teams or unrelated workstreams in large repos
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "The claudeMdExcludes setting lets you skip specific files by path or glob pattern... Add it to .claude/settings.local.json so the exclusion stays local to your machine." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Choose a token-efficient output style (avoid Explanatory or Learning for production use)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Output styles modify the system prompt and control verbosity of every response. The built-in Explanatory and Learning styles produce longer responses by design (adding 'Insights' sections and TODO markers). Default style is the most token-efficient for coding tasks. Custom styles with keep-coding-instructions: false drop Claude Code's built-in software engineering system prompt entirely when not coding.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json — set key `outputStyle` to `"Default"`, `"Proactive"`, `"Explanatory"`, `"Learning"`, or a custom style name. Alternatively run `/config` and select Output style.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Switching from Explanatory or Learning to Default eliminates extra output tokens per response; using a custom style without keep-coding-instructions saves system-prompt input tokens on non-coding tasks
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "The built-in Explanatory and Learning styles produce longer responses than Default by design, which increases output tokens." and "Token usage depends on the style. Adding instructions to the system prompt increases input tokens, though prompt caching reduces this cost after the first request in a session." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Set outputStyle at session start, not mid-session
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Output style is part of the system prompt layer, read once at session start. Changing it mid-session via /config does not apply until /clear or restart — and does not invalidate the cache. Changing it between sessions (before the first turn) avoids a cache miss. Changing it mid-session wastes the setting change until reset.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json — set `outputStyle` before starting a session; or run /config before the first turn
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents unnecessary cache invalidation; ensures the output style change actually takes effect
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Output style is part of the system prompt, which Claude Code reads once at session start. Changes take effect after /clear or a new session." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use /btw for side questions that should not enter conversation history
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: The /btw command shows an answer in a dismissible overlay without inserting the question or answer into the conversation history. This prevents side questions from growing context and consuming tokens on subsequent turns.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Interactive session command: /btw <question>
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Keeps context window clean of side questions; eliminates tokens for questions that are informational only
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "For quick questions that don't need to stay in context, use /btw. The answer appears in a dismissible overlay and never enters conversation history, so you can check a detail without growing context." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use /clear between unrelated tasks
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Stale conversation history from a previous task consumes tokens on every subsequent message in the same session. /clear resets the context window entirely, so the next task starts with only the base system prompt and CLAUDE.md. This also resets the prompt cache (project context reloads from disk).
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Interactive session command: /clear
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Eliminates stale conversation tokens from prior tasks on every turn of the new task
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Use /clear to start fresh when switching to unrelated work. Stale context wastes tokens on every subsequent message." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Customize /compact instructions to preserve only essential context
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Auto-compaction summarizes conversation history when approaching context limits. Without instructions, the summary may include irrelevant history. Custom compaction instructions (in CLAUDE.md or inline) tell Claude what to prioritize in the summary, producing a smaller, more focused summary and a smaller post-compact context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: CLAUDE.md — add a section `# Compact instructions` with what to preserve. Or inline: `/compact Focus on code samples and API usage`
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Produces smaller post-compaction context; avoids preserving irrelevant tokens across the compaction boundary
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Add custom compaction instructions: /compact Focus on code samples and API usage tells Claude what to preserve during summarization." and "Customize compaction behavior in CLAUDE.md with instructions like 'When compacting, always preserve the full list of modified files and any test commands'" — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use subagents to isolate high-volume file reads and verbose operations
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Running tests, fetching documentation, grepping large logs, or exploring a codebase reads many files and generates large tool outputs — all of which accumulate in the main context window. Delegating these to subagents keeps the verbose output in the subagent's separate context; only a summary returns to the main conversation.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Inline prompt instruction: 'Use subagents to investigate X' — or define a subagent in .claude/agents/<name>.md with specific tools and model
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents large file-read and tool-output tokens from entering and remaining in the main context window
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Delegate these to subagents so the verbose output stays in the subagent's context while only a summary returns to your main conversation." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Prefer CLI tools over MCP servers for external service interactions
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: MCP tool definitions are loaded into the system prompt layer (even when deferred, the deferred tools' names take space). CLI tools like gh, aws, gcloud, sentry-cli are invoked via Bash and add zero per-tool listing tokens to context.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: CLAUDE.md — add instructions directing Claude to use specific CLI tools. Ensure CLI tools are installed in the environment.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids MCP tool-definition tokens in system prompt; also avoids cache invalidation when MCP servers connect/disconnect
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Prefer CLI tools when available: Tools like gh, aws, gcloud, and sentry-cli are still more context-efficient than MCP servers because they don't add any per-tool listing." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Disable unused MCP servers
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Each connected MCP server contributes tool definitions to the system prompt layer (or deferred tool name listings). Servers that are connected but not used still consume context tokens and can trigger cache invalidation when they connect/disconnect or push dynamic tool updates.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Run /mcp in session to see configured servers; disable via /mcp command or remove from .claude/settings.json `mcpServers` key
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Removes unused MCP tool definition tokens from context; prevents inadvertent cache invalidation from idle servers
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Disable unused servers: Run /mcp to see configured servers and disable any you're not actively using." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use hooks to preprocess large tool outputs before Claude sees them
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A PreToolUse hook can intercept Bash or other commands and rewrite them to filter output before it enters context. For example, a hook can grep a 10,000-line log file for ERROR lines and return only those, reducing context from tens of thousands of tokens to hundreds.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.json — add to `hooks.PreToolUse` array: `{"matcher": "Bash", "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "path/to/filter-script.sh"}]}`
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Can reduce large command-output tokens (e.g., test logs, build output) by orders of magnitude before they enter the context
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Instead of Claude reading a 10,000-line log file to find errors, a hook can grep for ERROR and return only matching lines, reducing context from tens of thousands of tokens to hundreds." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Pick model and effort level at session start — avoid mid-session changes
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Both model and effort level are part of the cache key. Switching either mid-session invalidates the entire prompt cache, causing the next turn to reprocess the full conversation history at full uncached input token cost. Setting them once at the start of a session maximizes cache hits throughout.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: /model command or `model` key in .claude/settings.json; /effort command or `effortLevel` key in .claude/settings.json
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents full cache invalidation mid-session; avoids paying full uncached input token rates on a long conversation
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Pick your model and effort level at the top of a session, then save /compact for natural breaks between tasks. The fewer changes you make mid-task, the higher your cache hit rate." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Enable 1-hour prompt cache TTL on API key / Bedrock / Vertex
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: By default, prompt cache entries on API key and third-party providers expire after 5 minutes of inactivity. Setting ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1 extends this to 1 hour. This keeps the cache warm through longer breaks and avoids paying full uncached input rates after stepping away from a session.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Environment variable: ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1 — set in shell or in managed-settings.json `env` block
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids full-context reprocessing costs when resuming a session after a break longer than 5 minutes but under 1 hour
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "On an API key, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, or Claude Platform on AWS, you pay the per-token rates, so the TTL stays at the cheaper five minutes by default. To opt into the one-hour TTL, set ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Reduce extended thinking budget for simple tasks
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Extended thinking is enabled by default and thinking tokens are billed as output tokens. The default budget can be tens of thousands of tokens per request. Lowering the effort level (/effort) or setting MAX_THINKING_TOKENS reduces thinking token spend on tasks that do not require deep reasoning.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Session command /effort to set effort level; env var MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=8000 for models with a fixed thinking budget; or disable thinking entirely in /config. Key `effortLevel` in .claude/settings.json.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Reduces output tokens (billed as output) on turns that don't need extended reasoning
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Thinking tokens are billed as output tokens, and the default budget can be tens of thousands of tokens per request depending on the model. For simpler tasks where deep reasoning isn't needed, you can reduce costs by lowering the effort level with /effort or in /model, disabling thinking in /config, or, on models with a fixed thinking budget, lowering the budget with MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=8000." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use Sonnet (not Opus) as the default model; switch to Opus only for complex tasks
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Opus costs significantly more per token than Sonnet. Sonnet handles most coding tasks well. Using Sonnet as the session default and switching to Opus only for complex architectural decisions keeps the per-session cost lower.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: `model` key in .claude/settings.json — set to `claude-sonnet-4-5` or equivalent current Sonnet ID; or use /model command. For subagents, set `model: haiku` in .claude/agents/<name>.md for simple tasks.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Reduces per-token cost for routine coding turns
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Sonnet handles most coding tasks well and costs less than Opus. Reserve Opus for complex architectural decisions or multi-step reasoning. For simple subagent tasks, specify model: haiku in your subagent configuration." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use /rewind instead of /compact when abandoning a wrong path
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: /compact produces a summary then rebuilds a new conversation cache. /rewind truncates back to an earlier turn whose prefix is already cached — so the next request hits the existing cache entry rather than re-computing. This is cheaper when you want to abandon a direction entirely rather than compress and continue.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Session command: /rewind (or double-tap Escape) — select a checkpoint from the menu
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids cache rebuild cost of /compact; reuses the already-cached prefix at the rewind point
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "If you've gone down a path you want to abandon entirely, /rewind to an earlier turn instead. Rewinding truncates back to a prefix that is already cached, rather than building a new one as compaction does." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Do not change output style mid-session (change takes effect after /clear or restart)
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Changing outputStyle mid-session via /config does not update the system prompt currently in memory and does not invalidate the cache. The change is silently deferred. If you need a different style, change it at the start of a new session or after /clear, not in the middle of a task.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: .claude/settings.local.json key `outputStyle` — set before starting a session; runtime: /config > Output style then /clear
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents confusion from settings that appear to take effect but don't; prevents wasted turns due to unexpected style mismatch mid-session
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Output style is part of the system prompt, which Claude Code reads once at session start. Changing it via /config or the outputStyle setting mid-session does not invalidate the cache, but the change also doesn't apply." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Avoid resuming long sessions after a Claude Code upgrade
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: A Claude Code upgrade typically changes the system prompt or tool definitions. Resuming a session after an upgrade reprocesses the entire conversation history at full uncached cost because the history now sits behind a different system prompt. The cost scales with session length.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Set DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1 in environment or managed-settings.json `env` block to control when upgrades apply. After upgrading, start a fresh session rather than resuming a long one.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Avoids paying full uncached input token rates on long session histories after an upgrade
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "Resuming a session after an upgrade reprocesses the entire conversation history with no cache hits, since the history now sits behind a different system prompt. The cost scales with how long the resumed conversation is." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Enable MCP tool search (deferred tool loading) — ensure it is not disabled
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: By default, MCP tool definitions are deferred: only tool names enter the system prompt layer until Claude actually uses a tool. When tool search is unavailable or disabled (e.g., on Haiku models, Vertex AI, or with a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), all tool definitions load into the system prompt prefix and any change invalidates the full cache. Keeping tool search enabled (the default) keeps definitions out of the cached prefix.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Default behavior — no action needed. Do NOT set alwaysLoad on MCP servers unless necessary. Do NOT use Haiku as main model if MCP tool count is high. Check /context to see current MCP overhead.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents MCP tool definitions from bloating the system prompt layer; prevents cache invalidation on MCP server changes
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "MCP tool definitions are deferred by default, so only tool names enter context until Claude uses a specific tool." and "Deferred tools, the default on supported models: a server connecting, disconnecting, or changing its tool list only appends new content and doesn't disturb anything already cached." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-caching
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context

research_knobs_actionable.name: Use auto memory instead of CLAUDE.md for things Claude discovers itself
research_knobs_actionable.mechanism: Auto memory (MEMORY.md in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/) stores Claude-discovered learnings. Only the first 200 lines or 25KB of MEMORY.md load at session start; detailed topic files load on-demand. This keeps auto-accumulated context separate from your hand-authored CLAUDE.md and automatically caps the base context contribution.
research_knobs_actionable.config_location: Auto memory is on by default. Toggle: `autoMemoryEnabled` key in .claude/settings.json or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1 env var. Custom location: `autoMemoryDirectory` key in settings.json.
research_knobs_actionable.saves: Prevents Claude-discovered learnings from bloating CLAUDE.md; topic files with detailed notes don't load at session start — only on demand
research_knobs_actionable.evidence: "The first 200 lines of MEMORY.md, or the first 25KB, whichever comes first, are loaded at the start of every conversation. Content beyond that threshold is not loaded at session start. Claude keeps MEMORY.md concise by moving detailed notes into separate topic files." — https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory
research_knobs_actionable.area: Claude Code CLAUDE.md and output styles — keeping CLAUDE.md small, output styles, and what bloats every-turn context
